title: lifecycle_class annotation
description: Write-time annotation schema for AI agent data stores. Declares retention basis, data-subject linkage, compliance obligations, and erasure behavior at record creation time.

lifecycle_class.description: Primary classification of what the record contains and how it behaves during deletion sweeps.

compliance_anchor.description: Overrides lifecycle_class for deletion decisions when the record has conflicting retention obligations (e.g. an audit row that is both identity-linked and compliance evidence).

basis.description: Legal or regulatory basis for the compliance hold (e.g. 'GDPR Art.12 erasure compliance evidence').

retain_until.description: ISO 8601 timestamp after which normal lifecycle_class deletion rules apply. All compliance holds must be finite.

subject_chain.description: Links a derived representation to the data subjects whose data contributed to it. Required for learned_context records and any identity record that will generate downstream derived representations. Enables tractable Art.17 erasure of embeddings, summaries, and propagated copies.

subjects.description: One-way hashes (sha256: prefix) of data subject identifiers present in the source data. Do not store raw identifiers here.

source_records.description: UUIDs or record identifiers of source records that this derived representation was built from.

derived_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this derived representation was created.

downstream_processors.description: Agent IDs or processor identifiers that received copies of this data. These processors must be notified on erasure cascade.

write_seal.description: Marks a proof-of-action artifact as immutable for the purpose of summarization and compaction. The record must be preserved in original form or explicitly archived — it must not be summarized, compressed, or rewritten by context management operations.

sealed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this record was sealed.

action_ref.description: Reference to the transaction, task, or action this record proves (e.g. a transaction UUID, task ID, or event identifier).

seal_basis.description: Human-readable description of why this record must not be summarized (e.g. 'regulated action proof-of-completion', 'DSAR compliance evidence').

summarization.description: 'prohibited' means the record must never be summarized or rewritten. 'archive-only' means it may be moved to cold storage but not compressed or paraphrased.

hash.description: Optional sha256: hash of the sealed content for tamper detection.
